title: uxdatagrid
text: uxdatagrid The uxdatagrid is an entity-level widget that shows tabular entity data as a set of rows and columns. The uxdatagrid allows users to quickly and easily look up values that indicate some kind of connection between different types of data, for example name of an employee in an organization and their salary, or a day of the week, or the timetable for a local swimming pool. The uxdatagrid widget shows occurrences as rows and fields as cells (columns). The collection of occurrences is determined by filtering applied onto the widget, where filtering can be applied procedurally, typically in the entity's read trigger. The filtering is needed to show the collection of occurrences in a certain context of the application. The first row of a uxdatagrid indicates the header row or simply called a table header. Usually the table headers are styled different from the rest of the table rows like having a separate background color or cell values in bold etc. This grid widget shows occurrences in the order as they are retrieved from the database or created using ProcScript. Sorting is implemented using the order by feature of the read statement or using the sort statement. Functional overview Data occurrences are displayed as rows of the data grid. Data might be sorted, as returned by read order by in the read trigger. Fields are displayed as cells/columns of the data grid in the order as defined in the data structure. Field values are displayed as formatted text where the actual formatting is provided by the field widget (only UX widgets). The actual field widgets are not shown. The data-grid comes with two types of horizontal responsiveness, as defined by the responsive-type property: wrap : Cell content wraps over multiple lines inside the cell when the data-grid, and therefore its cells, shrinks horizontally. horizontal-scroll (default): Cell content does not wrap and a horizontal scrollbar is added when the data-grid shrinks horizontally. The data-grid shows a vertical scrollbar when the data-grid shrinks vertically. The widget is read-only. Fields/columns/cells can be excluded from the data-grid by setting the exclude field property to true. The data-grid has its own label text as specified by the label-text entity property. Column labels are specified by the label-text field property. Limitations Column properties, like labels, can only be defined as declarative field properties, either using PI of field or using $properties(field). To change column properties dynamically, use collection properties: Explain this code putitem/id $curentproperties(<ENTITY>), "field-<FIELD>.<ENTITY>.<MODEL>:label-text", "<new-label-text>" Copy Label text as defined in the Model are not supported. No interactive sorting. No paging. No editing abilities. Triggers The uxdatagrid widget does not fire any trigger. Extends fluent-data-grid Web Component This widget is implemented using the Fluent web components fluent-data-grid , fluent-data-grid-row and fluent-data-grid-cell . Most of its features are also available in these widgets. For more information, see the Fluent documentation on the fluent-data-grid web component. This also means that the uxdatagrid widget, like all other UX widgets, follows the Fluent design system. Keyboard support The uxdatagrid is a single focusable object within the tab sequence of the web page. So, (shift) TAB key can be used to navigate in and out the uxdatagrid . When tabbing into the uxdatagrid , the last selected cell gets selected. Once the focus is within the uxdatagrid , the arrow keys are used to navigate the cells. Implementation Details and Layout processing The uxdatagrid consists of the following four JS widget classes: UX.DataGridCollection : Implements the collection-level part of the data-grid widget. Its processLayout() method generates the fluent-data-grid root element, iterates over the component structure and generates a column header placeholder element for every field in the component structure, and a placeholder element for the occurrence widget. UX.DataGridColumnHeaders : Implements the column header part of the data-grid widget. Its processLayout() method generates a fluent-data-grid-cell element with a single slotted element that is bound to the column label. UX.DataGridOccurrence : Implements the collection-level part of the data-grid. Its processLayout() method generates the fluent-data-grid-row element for the row which gets bound to the occurrence, iterates over the component structure and generates a placeholder element for the field widgets. UX.DataGridField : Implements the field-level part of the data-grid or, in other words, the cell part. Its processLayout() method generates the fluent-data-grid-cell element for the field which gets bound to the field. The widget transparently listens to all properties of the field that might affect the textual representation of the field value inside the cell. This typically includes the field value itself, the valrep, and other value formatting properties like the display-format property. The exact list of properties is determined by the field widgets. At runtime, the original field widgets as specified in the component structure of the IDE, are replaced by the UX.DataGridField . So, inside the data-grid it is the UX.DataGridField widget that shows the cell content, fires triggers, shows errors; all in a data-grid-cell specific way. The UX.DataGridField widget is not available as a field widget in the IDE, simply because it cannot work outside the context of the UX.DataGridCollection and UX.DataGridOccurrence widgets. Simply assign one of the UX field widgets, and the UX.DataGridField will communicate with that widget for value formatting. Cell content value and formatting The UX.DataGridField widget has the ability to show various aspects of the original field widget as part of the cell content. This is done by calling out to the original field widget with a request to provide this information. First, the cell widget invokes the static method getFormattedValueSetters() . This static function must return an array of property names on which the cell widget needs to react to refresh the cell content, typically value , valrep , and uniface:display-format . Second, the cell widget invokes the static method getFormattedValue() . This static function must return an object with the information to be shown by the cell. All field widgets must implement these methods if they want to influence how they get represented by the cell of the data-grid widget. By default, only the field value and a validation error visualization is shown. Return object.item Description primaryPlainText Defines the primary cell content as plain text. Typically used to show the non-interpreted field value in a secure way. primaryHtmlText Defines the primary cell content as HTML. Typically used to show an interpreted or representation of the field value. This option is ignored if primaryPlainText is defined. Note: Be careful while using this option. Any script tags or other embedded script might get executed by this option. Therefore, never use this to show the non-interpreted field value, which makes your application sensitive for injection attacks. secondaryPlainText Defines the secondary cell content as plain text. By default, the secondary text follows the primary text inline and within parentheses. It is typically used to show the non-interpreted field value in a secure way when this would be relevant to the end-user. It is typically combined with field representation as primary text. secondaryHtmlText Defines the secondary cell content as HTML. This option is ignored if secondaryPlainText is defined. Note: Be careful while using this option. Any script tags or other embedded script might get executed by this option. Therefore, never use this to show the non-interpreted field value, which makes your application sensitive for injection attacks. prefixText Defines a piece of plain text that needs to be shown in front of the primary/secondary cell content. Ignored if prefixIcon is defined. prefixIcon Defines the name of the icon, as it exists in the ui-fabric-core library, that needs to be shown in front of the primary/secondary cell content. suffixText Defines a piece of plain text that needs to be shown after of the primary/secondary cell content. Ignored if prefixIcon is defined. suffixIcon Defines the name of the icon, as it exists in the ui-fabric-core library, that needs to be shown after the primary/secondary cell content. errorMessage Defines the error message that the cell needs to show as an error. Typically used for a validation or formatting error message. Note: The styling of each part can be changed by following the CSS styling selectors described below. When applying custom widget styling, make sure to honor the responsive-type property. Entity-level widget properties Property Description Dynamic update using ProcScript or JavaScript label-text Defines the label text of the data-grid as it is shown in the header section of the data-grid. Explain this code putitem/id $curentproperties(ENTITY), "label-text", "text" Entity.setProperty("label-text", "text") Copy responsive-type Defines how the data-grid responds to size changes: wrap : The cell content wraps over multiple lines inside the cell when the data-grid, and therefore its cells, shrinks horizontally horizontal-scroll (default): The cell content does not wrap and a horizontal scrollbar is added when the data-grid shrinks horizontally Explain this code putitem/id $curentproperties(ENTITY), "responsive-type", "wrap" Entity.setProperty("responsive-type", "wrap") Copy html:title Defines the tooltip of the data-grid which is shown when hovering any area of the data-grid. Explain this code putitem/id $curentproperties(ENTITY), "html:title", "text" Entity.setProperty("html:title", "text") Copy html:hidden Defines whether the data-grid is shown or hidden. false : Shows the data-grid (default) true : Hides the data-grid Note: This is only visual. Both the data-grid and all its data are still present in the client browser and included in client-server communication. Explain this code putitem/id $curentproperties(ENTITY), "html:title", "text" Entity.setProperty("html:title", "text") Copy Field:label-text Defines the label text of the column header associated to Field , where Field is specified by its fully qualified name prefixed with field- . Note: This property specifies the column text and not the individual label text of the field; the data-grid does not show label texts of individual fields. Explain this code putitem/id $curentproperties(ENTITY), "field-FIELD.ENTITY.MODEL:label-text", "Column header text" Entity.setProperty("field-FIELD.ENTITY.MODEL:label-text", "Column text") Copy Field:column-title Defines the tooltip of the column header associated to Field , where Field is specified by its fully qualified name prefixed with field- . Note: This property specifies the tooltip of the column header and not the tooltip of the cell of the field. Define the field property instead. Explain this code putitem/id $curentproperties(ENTITY), "field-FIELD.ENTITY.MODEL:column-title", "Column header tooltip" Entity.setProperty("field-FIELD.ENTITY.MODEL:column-title", "Column header text") Copy area-slot Explicitly assign widgets to specific slots. The options are: header main (default) footer - Field-level widget properties Property Description Dynamic update using ProcScript or JavaScript label-text Defines the label text of the column header associated to field. Not possible to update dynamically as a field property, set the property as a collection property instead. column-title Defines the tooltip of the column header associated to field. Not possible to update dynamically as a field property, set the property as a collection property instead. html:title Defines the tooltip of the cell associated to field. Explain this code putitem/id $fieldproperties(FIELD.ENTITY), "html:title", "Cell tooltip" Field.setProperty("html:title", "Cell tooltip") Copy exclude Defines whether the associated field is shown as a column in the data-grid. false - shows the field as a column in the data-grid (default) true - excludes the field as a column in the data-grid. Note: This is only visual; the field data is still present in the client browser and included in client-server communication. Not possible to update dynamically. This is a static property. Note: The UX.DataGridField widget transparently listens to all properties of the field that might affect the textual representation of the field value inside the cell. This typically includes the field value itself, the valrep, and other value formatting properties like the display-format property. The exact list of properties is determined by the field widgets. For more details, see the documentation of the various UX field widgets - Widget Reference: Dynamic Server Pages . Helper and validation error visualization The field level part of the uxdatagrid can show formatting errors as well as validation errors. In these cases, only one error icon is shown giving the validation error (tooltip text) message precedence over the formatting error. UI blocking As the uxdatagrid is read-only, it will not block user input when in transaction with the server. Widget styling CSS Selector Selected node(s) .u-datagrid-container Widget node .u-datagrid-container.u-hidden Widget node when the widget is hidden .u-datagrid-container .u-datagrid-header-toolbar Widget header nodes .u-datagrid Data-grid node .u-datagrid[u-responsive-type="horizontal-scroll"] Data-grid node when responsive-type property is set to horizontal-scroll . .u-datagrid[u-responsive-type="raw"] Data-grid node when responsive-type property is set to raw . .u-datagrid-header-row Header row node(s) .u-datagrid-header-cell Column header cell nodes .u-datagrid-header-cell.u-sw-field-fieldname-entname-modelname Column header node for the specified field (names are in lowercase) .u-datagrid-content-row Content row nodes for occurrences .u-datagrid-content-cell Content cell nodes for fields .u-datagrid-content-cell[id^="ufld:FIELDNAME.ENTNAME.MODELNAME] Content cell nodes for specific field (names are in uppercase) .u-datagrid-content-cell.u-read-only Content cell nodes for read-only fields .u-datagrid-content-cell.u-disabled Content cell nodes for disabled fields .u-datagrid-content-cell.u-hidden Content cell nodes for hidden fields .u-datagrid-content-cell.u-invalid Content cell nodes for fields with invalid data .u-datagrid-content-cell .u-prefix-icon Content cell prefix icon node .u-datagrid-content-cell .u-prefix-text Content cell prefix text node .u-datagrid-content-cell .u-value Content cell value node .u-datagrid-content-cell .u-value .u-text Content cell value text node .u-datagrid-content-cell .u-value .u-text .u-primary-text Content cell value primary text node .u-datagrid-content-cell .u-value .u-text Content cell value secondary text node .u-datagrid-content-cell .u-value .u-error-icon Content cell value error icon node .u-datagrid-content-cell .u-suffix-icon Content cell suffix icon node .u-datagrid-content-cell .u-suffix-text Content cell suffix text node Widget sources The widget source files, such as JavaScript and CSS files, can be downloaded from the following GitHub repository: https://github.com/RocketSoftware/uniface-ux/tree/main/src/ux/data_grid . Related Links ux-DataGrid This section provides description for the logical widget ux-DataGrid properties. UX Widgets Uniface UX provides a set of widgets that are implemented using the UX Widget Interface. This set is referred as UX Widgets. The sources of these widgets are available and allow changes and/or extensions to the widgets. In a default Uniface installation, you can find the sources in the <install>\uniface\webapps\uniface\common\ux folder. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
